page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 30 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top tiers. Memory performance is a key highlight, with both read-only and write large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks reaching the top 10% tier. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, also achieve top-tier performance. Software compilation with Clang and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are highly efficient on this architecture, also scoring in the top 10%.

The instance delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency due to its custom ARM64 architecture, which optimizes power and compute delivery per core compared to traditional x86 alternatives. However, the lack of hyperthreading and a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per core represent tradeoffs for memory-bound workloads. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-sized LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing 64 dedicated ARM64 cores on the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Running on the Nitro hypervisor at 2.8 GHz, it features 128.0 GB of RAM and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core processing performance, as demonstrated by strong Geekbench and Redis benchmarks. With a baseline network speed of 30 Gbps and no hyperthreading, it offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native deployments. This instance is optimized for high-throughput databases, multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge features 64 ARM64 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2
3800 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, high-throughput databases, and web serving
